# Oscar Mañas

> Research Scientist at Meta Superintelligence Labs (Media Generation team), Zurich.
> Multimodal AI researcher specializing in vision-language generative models —
> image, video, and text generation and understanding. Focus on efficiency of generative models.

## Current Role

Research Scientist, Meta Superintelligence Labs — Media Generation team, Zürich (Oct 2025 – present)
Working on generative model distillation and real-time interactive video generation.

## Previous Roles

- Visiting Researcher, Meta FAIR, Montreal (Jan 2024 – 2025). Multimodal vision-language understanding and generation.
- Research Scientist Intern, FAIR Labs, Meta AI, Montreal (Jun 2023 – 2023). Text-conditioned image generation.
- Research Intern, Element AI / ServiceNow, Montreal (2020 – 2021). Data-efficient ML and self-supervised visual representation learning.

## Education

- PhD Computer Science, Mila / Université de Montréal (2021–2025). Advisor: Prof. Aishwarya Agrawal. GPA 4.3/4.3. Thesis: "Towards efficient, reliable and measurable vision-language systems" (https://hdl.handle.net/1866/44263)
- MSc Computer Vision, Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona / Computer Vision Center (2018–2020). Rank 1/35.
- BSc Computer Science, Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (2013–2017). Rank 1/235.

## Research Expertise

- Video generation and efficient generative models
- Multimodal vision-language generative models (image, video, text)
- Multimodal LLMs and reward-guided decoding
- Diffusion models, distillation, real-time generation
- Contrastive and self-supervised representation learning
- Vision-language alignment and evaluation

## Publications (12 papers, sorted by arXiv submission date)

1. "LatentLens: Revealing Highly Interpretable Visual Tokens in LLMs" — arXiv Feb 2026. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.00462
2. "SneakPeek: Future-Guided Instructional Streaming Video Generation" — arXiv Dec 2025. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.13019
3. "Controlling Multimodal LLMs via Reward-guided Decoding" — ICCV 2025 (accepted Jun 2025). https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.11616
4. "Learning What Matters: Prioritized Concept Learning via Relative Error-driven Sample Selection" — CVPR 2026 (arXiv Jun 2025). https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.01085
5. "EvalGIM: A Library for Evaluating Generative Image Models" — arXiv Dec 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.10604
6. "Consistency, Diversity and Realism: Pareto Fronts of Conditional Image Generative Models" — arXiv Jun 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.10429
7. "An Introduction to Vision-Language Modeling" — arXiv May 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.17247
8. "Improving Text-to-Image Consistency via Automatic Prompt Optimization" — TMLR (accepted Jun 2024). https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.17804
9. "Improving Automatic VQA Evaluation Using Large Language Models" — AAAI 2024 (accepted Dec 2023). https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02567
10. "MAPL: Parameter-Efficient Adaptation of Unimodal Pre-Trained Models for Vision-Language Few-Shot Prompting" — EACL 2023. https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.07179
11. "Seasonal Contrast: Unsupervised Pre-Training from Uncurated Remote Sensing Data" — ICCV 2021. https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.16607
12. "A Weakly Supervised Consistency-based Learning Method for COVID-19 Segmentation in CT Images" — WACV 2021. https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.02180

## Selected Awards

- J. Armand Bombardier Excellence Grant, Université de Montréal (2024, 2025)
- DIRO Excellence Grant, Université de Montréal (2023, 2024)
- National End-of-Degree Award in University Education, Spanish Ministry of Education (2021)
- Mila PhD Scholarship, Mila - Quebec AI Institute (2021)
- Mitacs Accelerate International Grant, Mitacs Canada (2020)

- Spotlight talk, Deep Learning Barcelona Symposium 2025 (Dec 2025). Recording: https://www.youtube.com/live/Xz7Est3JlBw?si=ZIT4hey-Zy6vm30n&t=9521
